findings.evidence: `rg -n "fiber.*stabilizer|stabilizer.*fiber|stabilizer.*conj|conj.*stabilizer" code/TauCeti` finds only `Deck.Connected.fiber_stabilizer_eq_bot` at `code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/Deck/Connected.lean:96`; the generic ingredients exist in Mathlib at `Mathlib/GroupTheory/GroupAction/Basic.lean:252` and `:262`, but are not packaged in the deck-fibre language needed by the roadmap target.
findings.fix: Add deck-local wrappers such as `mem_fiber_stabilizer_iff`, an underlying-coe form, orbit/conjugacy equivalences built from `MulAction.stabilizerEquivStabilizer`, and a `fiberMap`/`conjMulEquiv` transport lemma, with `@[simp]`/`@[grind]` on the normal forms.
findings.issue: The public surface lacks the characteristic stabilizer API for fibre points: there is no deck-local membership/underlying-point characterization, no conjugacy equivalence for stabilizers of points in the same deck orbit, and no transport lemma along `fiberMap`.

summary: The PR does not expose the deck-fibre stabilizer API it claims to add; downstream users still have to work directly with raw `MulAction.stabilizer` and generic Mathlib conjugacy lemmas.
